How do you fire people that aren't right for your business? You first have to identify that they're not the right people. After you successfully identify that it's time to let somebody go, you have to understand this is going to be a non-emotional thing for you. You can't get emotionally charged as the individual that you're firing becomes emotionally charged from you letting them go. Meaning as soon as you sit down and you're like, hey Joe, you know, it's been great. I love the work that you've done, but I just, I just got to ask. I mean, it doesn't seem like your head's in the game as much as it was. Let them answer. You know, I can't help but think that you're just not that happy compared to what you could be. It just doesn't seem like this is your game. No, I love it. This is my game. I absolutely don't know what you're talking about. Or they'll be like, you're right, this just isn't my thing as much. And you're like, you know, what I'd recommend is just for the purpose of reputation's sake. I mean, I could either let you resign or I could fire you, but I'd recommend, you know, obviously resigning if that's the path that you want to go. So you're not happy and that it's not your game anymore. Would you like to take the opportunity to let yourself go? Yeah, I think this isn't for me. You know, that's the best case scenario. Okay. Here's the worst case scenario. You say something like, all right, here's the middle case scenario. So you're like, hey, you know, Joe, I just want to sit you down real quick, man. It doesn't seem like this is, this is your game anymore. You know, it doesn't seem like you're, you're that in it as much as you were. Well, no, I don't know what you're talking about. I mean, I love my job a minute every day. You know, this is my favorite thing. You know, Joe, I just, I wanted you to know that this is something I think just that's making you unhappy. You seem unsettled and you seem like this isn't something that's getting you the fulfillment that at one point you thought it was going to. Jeremy, I don't know what you're talking about. I'm really confused. You know, I really love my job here. It really seems like I'm, I mean, I have a great time. I love all the people I love. Okay, well, Joe, hold on one second, man. Let me explain something. You know, I just want to let you know, I've been looking at your statistics and I sat you down over the last two weeks, because you should sit them down the week before and say, Hey, your stats are going down, what's going on? And you should sit them down as soon as you notice it two weeks before and say, Hey, I noticed your stats are going down. What are you going to do to fix it? You see, but the end of week two of their stats are still down. That's how you're able to fire somebody is because their stats have gone down and not emotionally made a decision that they're not performing. Or obviously, they did an HR infringement that requires you to fire them. It's never over the fact that you don't just flat out like the person that's going to open you up to getting yourself a lawsuit on your hands. It's a bad reputation on Glassdoor.com. That being said, if you've got this part in the conversation, the person's like, Oh, no, I love my job. I don't know what you're talking about. Well, Joe, let me ask you a question, man. I know that your stats are down and I know that we've talked about this the last two weeks. You're not in a good position inside of my company to be talking to me about liking everything that's going on because your stats are down, man. You know, it's it's something I've taken a look at and I've asked you over the last two weeks to focus some attention on this and get your stats up. But you know, I just don't think it's working out for you or I think it's time that we're going to have to unfortunately let you go. And then Joe might be like, Oh, man, it sucks, dude. I got my family and all that stuff. Like, Oh, man, what's my wife going to say? Like, Joe, listen, I just want you to know that here at Menklejohn Margining, we appreciate all the work that you did. And we're here to give you any kind of referral recommendation moving onward. If you need anything from us, however, we'd encourage you to go grab your stuff, escort yourself out. I can have the building come up and grab you if you'd like, but I encourage you, please go with these. And if there's anything that we can do for you in the future, we encourage you. Let us know. We'll be there for that letter of recommendation when you need it. Now, hopefully Joe gets out of your office and he doesn't. And you're in a physical environment. Get him the fuck out. If you're not in a physical environment, you're on the phone, he's probably going to hang up on you. He's probably going to go cry or go upset. However, if you have the worst case scenario occur, which sounds more like this, hey, so Joe, you know, we've been looking at your stats over the last two weeks. And, you know, we've noticed that they've obviously gone down and they haven't an issue with this. And we just wanted to find out, you know, your head just doesn't seem like it's in the game that much, man. It doesn't seem like this is your game anymore. You're not not that happy or fulfilled. I just, I wanted to know what's going on and see if it's, see if it's for you anymore. You know, it seems like you want to let yourself go or something. Jeremy, I don't know what you're talking about. I don't know what you're sitting out. Absolutely love my job here. And I do a great job at it. I can't believe you have the disrespect to tell me that. Well, Joe, I've just been looking at your statistics. This is why statistics are important guys, because right here, if you have that employee that's emotionally going to banter at you, you need to have some kind of data that's like, Hey, you're fucking up and here it is. Okay, I have some employee sign, what's called a no report report. And I'll be sure that you guys have that download below what's in it. It's called the no report report. This is a report that you have your employees signed in real time when they don't finish anything. Meaning, if you tell somebody to do something and they don't do it, no report report. If you request a report and they don't give, they don't give it to you, no report report. They show up late to work. No report report. After you accumulate these things in your business, it's important, especially if you have W2 or 1099 employees for the purpose of firing them, that you have some kind of actual documentation that you sign and the they sign that's dated with an incident report on it. That is this no report report that represents when they fucked up and why and them acknowledging the fuck up. That way, when it comes time to fire, you can be like, Well, listen, Joe, I have all these no report reports here, and you're in a position where unfortunately, we've talked to you about this a few times, and you just went over the line again, and unfortunately, we're going to have to let you go. You could be like, Jeremy, fuck off, and all I didn't do, ah, and you got to be like Joe, and get him the fuck out, get him out of your office. That point obviously inspired an emotional mentor. I had a girl one time, she was my she was my executive assistant, I couldn't believe it. I treated this girl like a goddess in a sense, in a way that's appropriate for work. Let's get that going. I had made sure that she had a laptop, I made sure that she had all this company equipment, I invested a lot of my time in tour, I had done everything in order to make sure that this person was over the top, but personally, and in my business, to be secure, confident, and comfortable with her position. That being said, I had even increased her pay to a point where it was uncomfortable for me, but comfortable for her because she had a kid, and I wanted to as a friend make sure that she was supplemented past the point where I thought she would be struggling. So I had given her more revenue, and I had also given her more time and attention, and I allowed her to be around me more, but unfortunately, toxic people are toxic no matter what the environment. Long term, I saw her dwindle and I saw her mindset get focused on things like demons and other weird things that have no relevance to my business, or accomplishing anything that moves my life forward as my executive assistant, and came the time to let her go. How did I know it was time to let her go? It was after I had brought up the fact that I needed her to come over an hour early prior to the time that she needed to come over, and she decided to tell me 11 different times back and forth a different reason why she couldn't come. Each time that she'd say whatever bullshit excuse she'd come up with, I would immediately overcome it because it was an invalid set of reasoning. It'd be something like, well, Jeremy, I'm not going to be able to come over an hour early because I can't drive there. And I said, you told me yesterday you were going to uber. And she said, oh, well, and then she'd switch her excuse. I'm a good salesperson. When somebody doesn't have the one objection that they said, and they repeat it, that doesn't mean that that's not the real objection. Meaning if they say that this is the problem, it's not the actual problem if they rotate the actual problem. You see what I mean? If they say, I don't have any money, I don't have any money, I don't have any money, they actually don't have money. If they say I don't have any money, it's not the right time. I got to go talk to my wife. They're ready to buy, dude. Close them up. The same thing occurs when this woman is giving me an excuse. She's like, I can't do it for this, for this, for this, for this, for this. After the 11 time, I was like, you know what, get the fuck out of here. This is rough. I hate this. You are making me emotionally upset and you're draining me right now. I don't know why you're talking to your CEO like this. You're my executive assistant. If I need you to come over an hour early, you fucking come an hour early because that's what I'm requesting and your excuses are invalid. Give me a valid reason in which you can't do what I need you to do and I will consider it. If you are ever in a position where you are considering firing somebody, make sure first of all it's not emotional. I'm in at that exact moment after hanging up the phone, after literally saying, I'm going to hang up the phone because you're emotionally draining me. Immediately took a deep breath and I meditated. In that meditation, I'd come to the conclusion that I didn't need to fire this individual because she was harming my cheek. It came time where I needed to know how to fire somebody and exactly what I did was say, hey, I just want to go and get you near your stats are going down. Your overall tolerance and my tolerance for dealing with the stuff I've been talking about is severely going down and I want to bring up the fact that I don't think it's working out between you and I. All that being said, I just want to make it clear that if you need anything in the future, I'm here for you from a letter of recommendation perspective, but I'm going to have to let you go. This individual then decided to tell me that I wasn't a good leader at which point all of the friendship and everything else and the whole good letter of recommendation thing went right down the drain. All that being said, people will choose every single day of their life in order to not associate with you and give you bad juju and negativity just because you decided not to pay him anymore, even though you would still be their friend. All that being said, you have to remember there's a difference between friendship and there's difference between business and whoever it is that's in your business and whenever they're there, unemotionally fire people by having some guy, no report, report stacked up against them or data to show and represent that they're not doing something that you need them to do. All in all, this isn't a hard thing to do guys. How to fire people, go into it just fire them. They're not good for your business, especially when you have a team. My current guy who I have is a social media marketing manager, he's thought of my organization. I actually just went and I brought in somebody else all because this person was toxic to my other staff members. As a CEO, you have to hold a firm mentality that if you don't fire somebody, they're going to be a cancer throughout your organization and they're going to poison everybody else. It is an injustice to your team if you do not fire people because they need you to based on the fact that they need better team members in order to perform and be more productive for you. This is how to fire people in phase number seven of the team building and execution phase. I'll see you guys in the next video. Go fire somebody.
